content.full_text:
STATE OF CALIFORNIA                                    EDMUND G. BROWN JR., Governor

FAIR POLITICAL PRACTICES COMMISSION
1390 K Street
Sacramento 95814
(916) 322-5660
                                                                                    76-01-222

                                                          January 23, 1976


Mr. Roy E. June, City Attorney
City of Costa Mesa
P.O. Box 1200
Costa Mesa, California

Dear Mr. June:

     Thank you for your letter of January 14, 1976,
concerning the disclosure provisions of the Political
Reform Act. I have reviewed your letter with several
attorneys on the Commission staff and we have concluded
that, on the facts presented, your advice was correct.

     If you have any further questions, please feel free
to contact me.

                                        Sincerely,

                                        [signature]
                                        Ted Prim,
                                        Staff Counsel

TP:pvp


                                                                                    9

FOR CONSIDERATION

CITY OF COSTA MESA
CALIFORNIA 92626          P.O. BOX 1200

FROM THE OFFICE OF THE CITY ATTORNEY

January 14, 1976

Fair Political Practices Commission
1100 Kay Street
Sacramento, California 95814

Attention: Nancy Salzman, Counsel

Dear Ms. Salzman:

Two of the City Councilmen of the City of Costa Mesa have elected
not to run for office and a group of their friends within the
City have formed a committee to give them a testimonial dinner.

Each will be given a gift in the form of a gift certificate from
a travel agency in an amount estimated to be approximately $500.00
per outgoing councilman.

This committee does not fit the definition of a committee as set
forth in §82013 of the Government Code and I have tentatively
given an opinion that inasmuch as the proceeds of the testimonial
dinner will not be used for the payment of campaign obligations,
no necessity exists for its filing form 410 with the Office of
the Secretary of State. I have also tentatively given my opinion
that each of these City Councilmen leaving office will have to
declare on appropriate forms this gift and its source.

I am writing to you at this time to seek informal confirmation of
these tentative opinions.

I recognize the possibility that gift certificates may perhaps be
converted to cash and the cash in turn expended to repay political
campaign expenses, however, inasmuch as this committee does not
qualify under §82013, the areas of its responsibility are not al-
together clear. The testimonial dinner for these two outgoing
councilmen will take place well after the election and under cur-
rent plans the tickets to be sold to the general public for the
testimonial dinner will also be undertaken after the next election.

May we have a response at your earliest convenience.

Very truly yours,

Roy E. June
City Attorney

REJ:kmb

May we have a response at your earliest convenience.

Roy E. June

sections.question: Two of the City Councilmen of the City of Costa Mesa have elected not to run for office and a group of their friends within the City have formed a committee to give them a testimonial dinner. Each will be given a gift in the form of a gift certificate from a travel agency in an amount estimated to be approximately $500.00 per outgoing councilman. This committee does not fit the definition of a committee as set forth in §82013 of the Government Code and I have tentatively given an opinion that inasmuch as the proceeds of the testimonial dinner will not be used for the payment of campaign obligations, no necessity exists for its filing form 410 with the Office of the Secretary of State. I have also tentatively given my opinion that each of these City Councilmen leaving office will have to declare on appropriate forms this gift and its source.
sections.conclusion: I have reviewed your letter with several attorneys on the Commission staff and we have concluded that, on the facts presented, your advice was correct.
sections.question_synthetic: Does a testimonial dinner committee for outgoing city councilmen need to file Form 410 with the Secretary of State, and must the councilmen receiving gift certificates disclose these gifts on appropriate forms?
sections.conclusion_synthetic: The FPPC confirmed that the City Attorney's tentative opinions were correct: the testimonial dinner committee does not require Form 410 filing, and the outgoing councilmen must declare the gifts and their sources on appropriate forms.
sections.parsing_notes: No section headers found; LLM: Document includes both incoming request letter (January 14, 1976) and FPPC response letter (January 23, 1976). FPPC response is brief confirmation letter without detailed separate conclusion section. Question text extracted from incoming request. Response indicates approval of City Attorney's tentative opinions.

QUESTION: Two of the City Councilmen of the City of Costa Mesa have elected not to run for office and a group of their friends within the City have formed a committee to give them a testimonial dinner. Each will be given a gift in the form of a gift certificate from a travel agency in an amount estimated to be approximately $500.00 per outgoing councilman. This committee does not fit the definition of a committee as set forth in §82013 of the Government Code and I have tentatively given an opinion that inasmuch as the proceeds of the testimonial dinner will not be used for the payment of campaign obligations, no necessity exists for its filing form 410 with the Office of the Secretary of State. I have also tentatively given my opinion that each of these City Councilmen leaving office will have to declare on appropriate forms this gift and its source.

CONCLUSION: I have reviewed your letter with several attorneys on the Commission staff and we have concluded that, on the facts presented, your advice was correct.

embedding.summary: The City Attorney of Costa Mesa sought FPPC confirmation regarding disclosure requirements for gifts given to outgoing councilmen at a testimonial dinner. The FPPC affirmed the City Attorney's opinions that Form 410 filing is not required but gift disclosure is mandatory.
